name: AWS WAF — Public API Burst Rate Limit
objective: Rate limit high-volume bursts on public API paths so scanning, brute-force enumeration, and accidental client storms are contained before they degrade backend capacity.
risk_mitigated: API enumeration, unauthenticated request floods, and sudden burst patterns that overwhelm origin systems or expose excessive object discovery surfaces.
potential_side_effects: Legitimate mobile app release spikes, integration retries, and partner batch jobs can trip a burst threshold if traffic baselines are not known.
tuning_notes: Pair this with client identity, API gateway quotas, and application-level auth protections. Start in Count mode and set endpoint-specific thresholds if one path receives materially more traffic than others.
deployment_notes: Attach to unauthenticated or partner-facing APIs before expensive backend workflows. Keep trusted internal paths, health probes, and callback endpoints outside the scope-down statement if they have separate controls.
monitoring_notes: Review CloudWatch metrics by resource and path, then correlate with ALB/API Gateway metrics, 4xx/5xx spikes, and upstream auth failures.
